sessions.intent: Translate the architect's 7-stage path-polymorphism design into C# TUnit test suites that define the behavioral contract for the coder.

before.assumptions: Architect plan is final (committed at fa703be9f). Post-runtime2 merge state: app.filesystem namespace still present, will move under app.types.path. Test folders under PLang.Tests/App/ stay PascalCase per CLAUDE.md.
before.risk: Large surface (7 stages). Stage 7 contract base is the centerpiece. HttpPath in-process server needs an ASP.NET/HttpListener dependency the test csproj does not yet have. Forward-stage tests referencing not-yet-existent types could break the whole test project.

actions.context: Batch 1 — Stage 1 (namespace move) survey tests + Stage 4 ([PathScheme] attribute). Survey tests specified as string-based reflection so they survive the old types being deleted; no compile-time typeof of a moved symbol.

Batches 2-3 — Stage 2: scheme registry Register/From, bare/schemed/unknown dispatch, multi-App isolation; abstract path hierarchy shape; FilePath verb round-trips; PLang type-mapper dispatch through the registry.

Batch 4 — Stage 3: survey tests asserting IFile/DefaultFileProvider/PLangFileSystem wrapper layer are absent and no [Code]-partial provider remains on file handlers, plus behavioural checks that the gate still fires from inside Path verbs. Existing Tests/Modules/File/File.test.goal is the PLang-level regression net (no new goal file).

Batches 5-6 — Stage 5 HttpPath (GET/POST/DELETE/HEAD, 404/405, identity, network error, HttpClient pooling) with an HttpTestServer skeleton (HttpListener-shaped, no new package); Stage 6 per-scheme Absolute canonical-form, one test per rule.

Batch 7 — Stage 7: the generic PathSchemeContractTests<TFixture> base (8 contract assertions), the IPathSchemeFixture interface + VerbName enum, FilePath/Http fixture skeletons, two [InheritsTests] concrete subclasses, and cross-scheme CopyTo. Contract files alias the path type to the CURRENT app.filesystem.path so the project compiles today; stage 1's rename sweep repoints them.

Decision: HttpPath in-process test server should use System.Net.HttpListener (in-box) rather than Kestrel, to avoid adding a Microsoft.AspNetCore.App framework reference. Flagged for the coder; no csproj edit made by test-designer.

after.status: Complete. 14 test files created under PLang.Tests/App/Types/PathTests/. 94 tests, all discovered and red ('Not implemented'). PLang.Tests builds with 0 errors.
after.health: Green build. All path-polymorphism tests in proper TDD red state. No existing test affected.
after.notes: Coder picks up next. Two non-blocking decisions surfaced: (1) use HttpListener for the test server (csproj: no new package needed); (2) HttpTestServer.cs and the two fixtures are skeletons (throw NotImplementedException) for the coder to implement in stages 5/7. Contract/ files intentionally alias the current app.filesystem.path — stage 1's rename sweep migrates them along with the existing PathAuthorizeTests/FileHandlerTests aliases.

sessions.intent: Implement the architect 7-stage path-polymorphism plan: scheme-polymorphic path, FilePath/HttpPath, handler collapse, contract tests.

before.assumptions: architect plan final; test-designer 94 red TDD tests in place; 2855 green baseline.
before.risk: Large surface. Stage 3 IFile removal touches many callers. No global Path alias possible (System.IO.Path clash).

actions.context: Stage 1 namespace move app.filesystem -> app.types.path; class path -> @this.
Stage 2 abstract path + FilePath + per-App scheme registry + type-mapper routing.
Stage 3 handler one-liners; IFile/DefaultFileProvider deleted; wrapper-layer removal flag-and-split.
Stage 4 [PathScheme] marker attribute.
Stage 5 HttpPath + Stage 6 canonical Absolute. Stage 6 also rewrote permission GlobMatches glob->regex.
Stage 7 contract framework (HttpTestServer, PathSchemeContractTests, fixtures) + all test-designer placeholder bodies.

after.status: All 7 stages implemented. C# 2875 pass / 1 deliberate red (wrapper-layer flag-and-split). PLang --test 203/203 pass.
after.health: Green except the one documented deferral red.
after.notes: Wrapper-layer (PLangFileSystem + Default/*) removal deferred — ~14 non-action App.FileSystem callers. No global Path alias (System.IO.Path clash). PLangPath test-alias name collision flagged for discussion with Ingi.

sessions.intent: Lowercase path aliases + Stage 8: remove the System.IO.Abstractions wrapper layer, per Ingi.

before.assumptions: Stages 1-7 landed (commit ae2206c9). PascalCase Path alias removed earlier due to System.IO.Path clash.
before.risk: IO.Abstractions removal touches ~18 production + ~20 test files. ValidatePath feared security-sensitive — turned out to be dead root-jail, now pure normalization.

actions.context: Lowercase global aliases path/filepath/httppath — no System.IO.Path clash (case-sensitive). Production call sites cleaned to use them.
ValidatePath relocated from PLangFileSystem as a static on filepath — pure path normalization, not a security gate (Authorize is). filepath.OsAbsolutePath exposes the os/ folder.

IPLangFileSystem + 9 System.IO.Abstractions wrapper types deleted. The root-jail (FileAccessControl) was already dead — replaced by Path.Authorize/Actor.Permission. Pure double-wrapping removed. System.IO.Abstractions NuGet package dropped.
~18 production callers migrated App.FileSystem.{File,Directory,Path} -> System.IO.*; IPLangFileSystem params -> app.@this. App.FileSystem property + %!fileSystem% context var removed.
~20 test files migrated off PLangFileSystem construction. FileSystemSurfaceShapeTests flipped to assert wrapper-layer absence. ValidatePathTests repointed to filepath.ValidatePath.

after.status: All 8 stages complete. C# 2875/2875 pass. PLang --test 202 pass / 0 fail / 1 stale.
after.health: Green. The 1 stale (ContextVars2.test.goal) is blocked by a pre-existing plang build breakage (builder.app->builder.load staleness in os/system/builder/, untouched on this branch, being fixed elsewhere by Ingi).
after.notes: Ready for codeanalyzer review. Two follow-up items for discussion: PLangPath test-alias name collision; filepath/httppath alias casing not finalized by Ingi.

sessions.intent: Five-pass codeanalyzer review of the 8-stage path-polymorphism branch.

before.assumptions: Coder reports all 8 stages complete, C# 2875/2875, plang 202/0/1-stale. No prior codeanalyzer version on this branch.
before.risk: Large surface (~6k lines). The polymorphism seams (handler dispatch, base/subclass split) are where shape errors hide; mechanical line-correctness can pass while the abstraction leaks.

actions.context: Reviewed the new app/types/path tree: abstract base, scheme registry, FilePath/HttpPath, Permission gate, Conversion dispatch. Registry is a clean @this type; gate centralisation genuinely closes v2 #1; GlobMatches rewrite correct. Flagged: base class keeps file-only live properties (Exists/Size) wrong for HttpPath; Relative ignores RootComparison; base Authorize references concrete file subclass.

Pass 1+4: 6 of 8 handlers downcast 'Path.Value is filepath fp' (F1) — polymorphism violation, FS-only options silently dropped for HttpPath. file.exists returns path vs bool per scheme (F3). Traced: unregistered scheme -> Path.Value null -> NRE inside Run() before the clean SchemeNotRegistered error can surface (F4).

Verdict NEEDS WORK. 8 findings: F1 handler downcast (High), F2 base file-semantics leak / F3 file.exists shape / F4 unregistered-scheme NRE (Med), F5-F8 (Low). Build clean, plang --test 202/0/1-stale verified on a clean rebuild.

after.status: Review complete. Verdict: NEEDS WORK (fail). Build green, tests green; findings are structural/correctness, not regressions.
after.health: Branch compiles and tests pass. The polymorphism abstraction leaks at the handler-dispatch and base/subclass seams — F1–F4 should be fixed before done.
after.notes: Headline: a branch named path-polymorphism has 6/8 handlers branching on concrete type. Fix is structural — lift FilePath's option-bearing verb overloads onto the abstract base as virtual. Next: coder addresses F1–F4.

sessions.intent: Address codeanalyzer v1 NEEDS-WORK findings F1-F8 on path-polymorphism (handler downcasts, base file-only semantics, file.exists shape, unregistered-scheme NRE).

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer report.md is an accurate diagnosis; build+tests green at branch HEAD (202 plang pass / 2875 C# pass). F1/F5/F6/F7/F8 have clear fixes; F2/F3/F4 need design decisions from Ingi.
before.risk: F2/F3 change file.exists return shape and base path surface — could break condition.if truthy coercion and existing exists tests. F4 touches the source generator.

actions.context: F6 — moved computed OsAbsolutePath onto app.@this; base Authorize and FilePath.ValidatePath now use it instead of reaching into the file subclass.

F1 — lifted option-bearing verbs (Delete/List/CopyTo/MoveTo/Save) onto the abstract path base; HttpPath implements them with FS-only options as documented no-ops; all 7 file handlers collapsed to one-liners, no concrete-type downcast.
F2 — moved file-only live properties Exists/Size off the base onto FilePath (they did System.IO calls wrong for HttpPath). F5 — Relative now uses RootComparison not hard-coded OrdinalIgnoreCase.

F3 — file.exists returns Data<Path>; path implements IBooleanResolvable.AsBooleanAsync (FilePath sync probe, HttpPath HEAD); Data.ToBooleanAsync dispatches; condition pipeline (Operator/IEvaluator/Default/if/elseif/compare) and assert IsTrue/IsFalse made async so  is correct (was always-true). Decided with Ingi.

F4 — As<T> InvokeResolve now catches a thrown Resolve (SchemeNotRegistered) and returns a failed Data<T> instead of letting the exception escape; file handlers guard with if (!Path.Success) return Path. Handler-level guard chosen by Ingi.
F7 — PathSchemeAttribute doc now names the Resolve(string,context) static factory contract. F8 — HttpPath.List/Mkdir route through AuthGate.

Migrated tests to async condition/assert surface and new verb signatures; added F4 unregistered-scheme handler test, AsBooleanAsync coverage for both schemes, if-exists true/false evaluator tests, and an abstract-surface test for the option-bearing verbs.

after.notes: All 8 codeanalyzer v1 findings addressed. C# 2881/2881 pass (+6 new tests). PLang 202 pass / 0 fail / 1 pre-existing stale (ContextVars2) — matches baseline, no regressions. Build clean.

sessions.intent: Re-review path-polymorphism after coder v3 addressed codeanalyzer v1 findings F1-F8.

before.assumptions: Coder v3 (commit eb85fcbd) claims all 8 v1 findings addressed; build clean, 2881 C# / 202 plang pass. F1 (lift option verbs to base), F3 (async truthiness pipeline + IBooleanResolvable), F4 (As<T> InvokeResolve catch) are the structural ones — verify they hold and ripple correctly.
before.risk: F3 turned the whole condition/assert pipeline async — wide ripple. file.exists became an identity passthrough; need to check it did not silently drop the authorization gate the old ExistsPathAsync carried.

actions.context: Re-reviewed coder v3 (eb85fcbd) against v1 F1-F8. Verified each fix by reading every changed production file, tree-wide grep for residual downcasts, and tracing the F3 async ripple. All eight genuinely fixed.

N1 (Medium): F3 refactor turned file.exists into an identity passthrough; old ExistsPathAsync gated on Read verb, new FilePath.AsBooleanAsync deliberately skips AuthGate. Out-of-root file-existence probing is now ungated, and asymmetric vs HttpPath.AsBooleanAsync which authorizes. Needs an explicit decision.

N2 (Low): path.Equals/GetHashCode still hard-code OrdinalIgnoreCase, same drift F5 fixed in Relative. N3 (Low): assert.ResolveTruthy duplicates the IBooleanResolvable dispatch instead of reusing Data.ToBooleanAsync().

after.health: Build clean (0 err). C# 2881/2881 pass. plang 201/203 pass (1 fail = external httpbin.org 502/503 outage, not code; 1 stale pre-existing).
after.notes: F1-F8 all genuinely fixed. One substantive new finding (N1 — silently-dropped file.exists auth gate) + two low (N2, N3). Next: coder addresses N1.

sessions.intent: Merge origin/runtime2 (fixed plang builder) into path-polymorphism.

before.assumptions: runtime2 builder fixes are wanted; merge-base is e30354a0f so the whole runtime2 builder rework comes in. v3 baseline: C# 2881 pass, PLang 202 pass / 0 fail / 1 stale.
before.risk: Path rework on this branch (filesystem.path -> path) collides with runtime2's path-resolution changes; runtime2's generator change may contradict this branch's thin-handler invariant.

actions.context: Sole merge conflict. Kept runtime2's rootRelative path-resolution fix, adapted type filesystem.path -> path (this branch's rename).

runtime2 commit 8166e753b re-added a try/catch around Run() in the generated handler. Ingi chose to keep it. Updated two stale Phase-3 thin-handler tests: HasNoTryCatchFinally -> WrapsRunInTryCatch (asserts try/catch present, no finally); CallsRunDirectly assertion relaxed to '__runResult = await Run();'.
Auto-merged cleanly from runtime2 (builder v3 rework, BuildGoal/BuildStep folder split, JsonString repair stages, Error.Details rendering). Verified build clean and tests green.

after.status: Merge complete. Build clean (0 errors). C# 2881/2881 pass. PLang --test 202 pass / 0 fail / 1 stale (pre-existing ContextVars2). Identical to v3 baseline.
after.notes: 9 Modules/Http/* tests are flaky (hit httpbin.org) — failed transiently on one run, passed on re-run. Not a merge regression.

sessions.intent: Address codeanalyzer v2 findings N1-N3 on path-polymorphism.

before.assumptions: F1-F8 confirmed fixed by codeanalyzer v2. N1 (file.exists lost AuthGate) is the substantive item; N2/N3 are low. Baseline: C# 2881/2881, PLang 203/203/0 stale.
before.risk: N1 adds AuthGate to AsBooleanAsync which the condition pipeline calls often — must stay free for in-root paths (IsInRoot) and only prompt out-of-root.

actions.context: N1: FilePath.AsBooleanAsync now routes through the gated ExistsAsync (mirrors HttpPath) instead of an ungated File.Exists. Restores the authorization gate F3 dropped; symmetric across schemes.
N2: path.Equals/GetHashCode switched from hard-coded OrdinalIgnoreCase to RootComparison — kills case-sensitivity drift on Linux.
N3: ResolveTruthy delegates IBooleanResolvable dispatch to Data.ToBooleanAsync instead of duplicating it; plain-value IsTruthy path kept.
New test proving the N1 gate (out-of-root denied -> false). Two IfExists condition tests given context-bearing in-root paths since AuthGate needs Context.Actor.

after.status: Complete. All 3 codeanalyzer v2 findings (N1/N2/N3) addressed.
after.notes: Build clean; C# 2882/2882; PLang --test 203/203/0 stale. N1 decision (gate it) confirmed with Ingi.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder's response to codeanalyzer v2 findings N1-N3 on path-polymorphism.

before.assumptions: Coder addressed N1-N3 in commit a1c3f9563; v2 verified F1-F8 already fixed.
before.risk: N1 is a permission-gate restoration — must confirm the gate actually fires and file/http behavior is symmetric, not just that a test passes.

actions.context: Verified N1 (FilePath.AsBooleanAsync routes through gated ExistsAsync, parity with HttpPath), N2 (Equals/GetHashCode use RootComparison), N3 (assert.ResolveTruthy delegates IBooleanResolvable dispatch to Data.ToBooleanAsync). All three genuinely fixed; deletion test + pass 1b clean; traced the IsInitialized guard in ToBooleanAsync and confirmed no behavioral divergence.
v3 review output. Verdict CLEAN.

after.status: CLEAN — N1-N3 all genuinely fixed, no new findings
after.health: Build clean (0 errors); C# 2882/2882; plang 203/203, 0 stale.
after.notes: path-polymorphism branch is sound. Next: tester.

sessions.intent: Address builder bot's 3-class self-rebuild regression report.

before.assumptions: Builder bot has low C# understanding — validated each claim. Class 1a (slash names) real; 1b (nested bare names) NOT a bug (error.handle stamps Step); Class 2 real LLM mis-compile; Class 3 unvalidated/intermittent.
before.risk: GetGoalAsync change touches dispatch resolution — must not regress bare-name or root-relative resolution.

actions.context: Class 1a: GetGoalAsync resolves slash-qualified names as {folder}/.build/{leaf}.pr with an ancestor walk; LoadFromFile leaf-matches a slash Name. Verified via self-rebuild — all 3 slash calls resolve.
ResolveGoalCallsInAction sets PrPath on resolved slash calls (LoadFromFile leaf-matches at dispatch). Did NOT add list<action> recursion — Class 1b validated as a non-bug (error.handle stamps Step).
Inverted File.Exists at builder bootstrap — fired 'no app' when the marker existed. Verified: self-rebuild now runs without --app=create.

Class 2 re-diagnosed: builder.actions has no Include param (Builder.Actions returns the full catalog); the builder bot's 'LLM drops Include' is wrong. Real defect is non-deterministic write-to mis-compile. Validator needs step text, which builder.validate lacks — escalated to Ingi.

after.status: Class 1a + inverted check done & verified via self-rebuild. Class 2 re-diagnosed (builder bot wrong); Class 2/3 pending an Ingi decision on validator placement.

sessions.intent: Validate test honesty for coder v5 (codeanalyzer v2 N1-N3 response) on path-polymorphism.

before.assumptions: Both suites green per coder/codeanalyzer; codeanalyzer v3 verdict CLEAN. Risk concentrates in review-driven fixes N2/N3 whose tests are most likely missing.
before.risk: A green 2882+203 suite can still hide false greens — vacuous tests and untested review-driven edits.

actions.context: Clean rebuild (stale-binary trap) then ran both suites: C# 2882/2882, plang 203/203/0 stale. Reproduces coder/codeanalyzer numbers, no regressions vs v5 baseline.
Read the path-polymorphism C# suite and deletion-tested the three v5 review-driven fixes (N1/N2/N3). N1 well covered; N2 and N3 have zero coverage.
Wrote 6 findings: 1 false-green (Assert.That(true)), N2+N3 missing-coverage, F3 plang negative branch missing, 2 weak assertions. Verdict needs-fixes.

after.health: Both suites green (C# 2882/2882, plang 203/203/0 stale), no regressions; feature C# coverage strong overall.
after.notes: Six test-honesty gaps. Costly two: review-driven v5 fixes N2 (path.Equals/GetHashCode) and N3 (assert path-truthiness) ship with zero coverage — reverting either keeps the suite green. Plus a vacuous Assert.That(true) test. All six are small/mechanical to close.
